originalTitle: One ant for $220: The new frontier of wildlife trafficking
title: Tiny Ants, Big Secrets in Kenya!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! I'm your kid-friendly news reporter, and I'm so excited to tell you about something happening in our amazing Africa, especially in Kenya. Today, we're going to talk about something very, very small, but also super important: ants! Can you believe that tiny ants are causing a big buzz? In Kenya, some people are trying to catch these small creatures and sell them for a lot, a lot of money. It's like a secret ant trade, and it's not a good thing for our tiny friends or for nature. Let's find out why!

Ants: Our Tiny Superheroes!

Ants are everywhere! You can find them marching in your garden, under a rock, or even in the cracks on the pavement. They might be small, but they are like tiny superheroes for our planet. They work really hard, all day long, helping our world stay healthy and happy. **Did you know?** Ants are super, super strong! Imagine carrying a car! Ants can carry things that are many, many times heavier than themselves. They are incredible! Ants live in huge families called colonies. Think of it like a whole city of ants, with thousands or even millions living together! Each ant has a special job. Some ants look for food, some clean their home, and some even take care of the baby ants. They are a very organized team. They also help plants grow bigger and stronger. How? By digging tiny tunnels in the soil, which helps air and water get to the plant roots. They also move tiny seeds around, helping new plants grow in new places. Ants are like little gardeners for the Earth!

A Big Secret About Tiny Ants

Now, here's the surprising part. Some people in Kenya are trying to catch these ants and sell them for a lot of money. We're talking about so much money for just one ant – like $220! That's enough money to buy many, many toys, or lots and lots of yummy ice cream! It's a huge price for such a tiny creature. These people are catching the ants from their natural homes in the wild. This is called 'wildlife trafficking' for tiny animals. It means taking animals away from where they belong and selling them secretly. It’s not allowed, and it's not fair to the ants or to our beautiful nature. Imagine someone coming to your house and secretly taking your pet without asking. It would be very sad, right? It's similar for the ants. Their home is in nature, with their families.

Why Do People Want These Special Ants?

You might be wondering, why would someone pay so much money for just one ant? Well, some people love to collect very special and rare things. These particular ants are very unique and beautiful, and they are hard to find. Sometimes, people want to keep these ants as 'pets' in special homes called 'ant farms'. An ant farm is like a clear box where you can watch the ants dig tunnels and live. It can be fun to watch ants, but taking them from their real home in nature causes problems. It’s better to watch ants that are already living freely outside, or to learn about them from books and videos. Their real home, where they can help nature, is the best place for them to be. **Did you know?** There are more than 12,000 different kinds of ants all around the world! Some have amazing colours, and some are very tiny while others are quite big!

How Does This Hurt Our Amazing World?

When people take ants from their homes in nature, it causes big problems, not just for the ant, but for everyone and everything around them. Each ant in a colony has a very important job. If one ant is taken away, the whole ant family suffers. Imagine if a part of your soccer team was missing. It would be harder to play, right? It's the same for the ants. If too many ants are taken, their colony can become weak and might not survive. Ants are also a part of a big food chain. Many other animals, like birds, lizards, and even some small mammals, eat ants to get their food. If there are fewer ants, these animals might not find enough to eat, which can make them hungry or sick. It’s like taking away food from other animals. Ants also help keep our soil healthy for plants. If there are fewer ants digging tunnels, the soil might not be as good for growing food for us and for animals. **Did you know?** Some plants actually need ants to carry their seeds to new places so that new plants can grow! It’s a special partnership!

What Can We Do to Help?

We can all be nature protectors, even us kids! We can do many important things to help our tiny ant friends: First, we can learn more about ants and how amazing and important they are. The more we know, the more we care! Second, if you ever see anyone trying to catch animals, especially in a secret or unfair way, you should tell a trusted grown-up right away. This could be your mom, dad, teacher, or another family member. They will know what to do. Third, remember to always leave animals in their natural homes. If you see a beautiful ant, just look at it, maybe take a picture with your mind, and let it stay safe in its home. We should never try to catch wild animals to keep as pets, especially if they are taken from nature. Let’s keep our eyes and ears open! We want all our tiny and big animal friends to be safe and happy in their homes across Africa. **Did you know?** Scientists study ants to learn how they work together so well! They use ant teamwork to think of new ideas for building robots or solving problems!

Let's Protect Our Tiny Friends!

Remember, every creature, no matter how big or how small, has a very special and important place in our amazing world, especially here in Africa. Ants might be tiny, but they play a super big role in keeping our Earth healthy. We need to protect them so they can keep helping our plants, our soil, and all the other animals. Let's promise to be kind to nature and all its wonderful animals, even the smallest ones! Together, we can make sure our ants stay safe and happy in their homes, doing their important work for all of us. Thank you for listening, and keep exploring the wonders of Africa!
summary: Some people in Kenya are secretly catching special ants and selling them for a lot of money, which is bad because these tiny ants are very important for our nature and should stay in their homes.
whyMatters: This matters because ants, even though they are small, are like superheroes for our planet; they help plants grow and feed other animals, so taking them from their homes hurts all of nature and breaks the rules about protecting wildlife.

title: Senegal parade Afcon trophy despite title controversy
content: Senegal parade the Africa Cup of Nations trophy before Saturday's friendly match against Peru in Paris - despite being stripped of the title earlier this month.

originalTitle: Dozens detained in Tel Aviv as anti-war protest turns violent
title: Kids for Peace: People Hope for Friendship Far Away!
content:
Hello, Peace Heroes!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter, Pendo, here from beautiful Africa! I hope you are having a super day, playing and learning with all your friends. Today, I want to tell you about something that happened very far away, in a big city called Tel Aviv. It's important for all of us to know what's happening in the wide world, even if it's far from our homes. We always want all children everywhere to be safe and happy, just like you are!

What Happened Far Away?

In a bustling city called Tel Aviv, which is located in a country far, far from our lovely Africa, some grown-ups gathered together. They were there because they felt a little worried about some big talks between different countries. Sometimes, countries don't always agree on everything, just like how you and your friends might sometimes disagree. These grown-ups really, really hoped that everyone could be friends instead of having big arguments or even big fights. They wanted to show that many people wished for peace and friendship for all. They were especially thinking about big countries far away, like the United States and Iran. These grown-ups in Tel Aviv wanted these big countries to be friendly and kind to each other. They hoped there would be no big problems or fighting between them. Their message was simple: 'Let's all be friends!' **Did you know?** A "city" is a very big town with lots of houses, shops, and roads! Think of a place like Accra in Ghana or Lagos in Nigeria – those are big, busy cities! Tel Aviv is a big city in a country called Israel.

What is a "Protest"?

When many, many people gather in one place to show what they think about something, we call it a "protest." It's like when you and all your friends want to play the same game, and you all cheer for it together! These grown-ups in Tel Aviv gathered because they deeply wanted peace. They held colourful signs with messages of hope and talked about how important it is for countries to be kind to each other. They wanted their voices to be heard by leaders and other people, hoping their strong message would help make things better and bring more peace to the world. A protest is a way for a group of people to say, "This really matters to us!" **Did you know?** People can protest in many peaceful ways! They might sing happy songs together, hold up bright, big signs with words like "Peace!" or "Friends!", or just stand together quietly to show they care. It's a powerful way to share a message!

Why Do People Want Peace?

Peace means everyone is happy, safe, and can live without fear. It means no fighting, no shouting, and lots of playing, sharing, and smiling. Imagine if you and your friends never fought, but always shared your toys fairly and played nicely together every single day. That's what peace is like, but for whole countries! These grown-ups in Tel Aviv wanted that kind of wonderful peace for all the big countries around the world. They hoped that all the leaders would choose to talk things out and be friends, so children everywhere, from here in Africa to far-off lands, could grow up safe, healthy, and sound. **Did you know?** When there is peace, children can go to school every day without worries! They can play freely with their friends, learn amazing new things, and dream big, big dreams for their future. Peace truly makes everything better for everyone in the whole wide world.

Who Helps Keep Us Safe?

During the gathering in Tel Aviv, there were also police officers. Police officers are very important people in any community. Their main job is to keep everyone safe and make sure that everyone follows the rules. They are like special helpers who protect us and keep order. They help us when we are lost, and they make sure that big gatherings, like this protest, stay peaceful and calm. If too many people gather in one spot, or if things get a bit too noisy or crowded, the police might politely ask people to move or go home. They want to make sure no one gets hurt and that everyone is safe. **Did you know?** Police officers often wear special uniforms so you can easily spot them in a crowd! They are there to help us feel secure and safe in our communities, whether it's in a busy city far away or right here in our vibrant villages in Africa.

Learning About Rules

In the news story, it mentioned that some people were "dispersed." This means the police gently asked them to go home or move to a different, less crowded place. It also said some people were "arrested." This means the police asked some people to come with them for a short talk, usually because a rule might have been broken during the gathering. It’s a bit like when your teacher asks you to take a time-out if you are not following classroom rules, to help everyone else learn and play nicely. The police make sure rules are kept so that everyone stays safe and fair interactions happen for all. **Did you know?** Rules are super important in life! They help us all live together happily, play fairly, and know what to do and what not to do. Even in your favourite games, you have rules, right? Like taking turns or not cheating! Rules make everything work smoothly.

Peace Around the World and in Africa

It's wonderful to know that people everywhere in the world, even far away in Tel Aviv, care so much about peace and friendship. Here in Africa, we also cherish peace very, very much! Our amazing continent has many different countries, and we all try our best to live together as good neighbours and friends. We share our beautiful lands and vibrant cultures. When there is peace, we can share our incredible stories, our joyful songs, and our amazing, delicious food with each other. We can visit different places, learn from new friends, and celebrate all the wonderful things that make us unique. Peace helps us all grow stronger together. **Did you know?** Africa is a very big continent with 54 different countries! Imagine all those different languages, cultures, and beautiful places to explore! We all share the same big sun and moon, and we all wish for a peaceful world.

You Can Be a Peace Hero Too!

Even though you are still small, you can be a peace hero every single day, right where you are! How, you ask? By sharing your colourful toys with your friends, by using kind and gentle words, and by always being ready to help others who might need it. When you see someone looking sad, you can give them a comforting hug or a warm smile. When you have a disagreement with a friend, you can talk it out calmly instead of shouting or getting upset. Every little act of kindness, every moment of sharing, and every spark of friendship helps build a peaceful world for all children, everywhere. So let's all be peace heroes, starting right now, today!
summary: In a faraway city called Tel Aviv, some grown-ups gathered to show they really wanted peace between countries, hoping everyone could be friends instead of fighting, and the police were there to keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: It matters because when people wish for peace, it reminds us that everyone, everywhere, wants to live in a safe world where countries are friends, and children can play and learn happily without worries, just like you.
